# Safety License Checklist: SPEC-023 - OCaml Language Support

**Purpose**: Validate that SPEC-023 safety and license requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable before implementation tasks are generated.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are grammar provenance requirements complete for source repository, package name, pinned version, git commit, npm integrity, license, and required implementation/interface WASM artifact names? [Completeness, Spec §Clarifications, Research §Vendor tree-sitter-ocaml, Data Model §OCaml Grammar Artifact]
- [ ] CHK002 Are required and non-required OCaml grammar artifacts distinguished clearly enough to include `tree-sitter-ocaml.wasm` and `tree-sitter-ocaml_interface.wasm` while excluding `tree-sitter-ocaml_type.wasm` unless a concrete need is planned? [Completeness, Spec §Clarifications, Research §Vendor tree-sitter-ocaml]
- [ ] CHK003 Are no-native-runtime-dependency requirements complete for both development-from-source and distributed runtime behavior? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Constraints, Constitution §VII]
- [ ] CHK004 Are no-networked-runtime-behavior requirements complete for grammar loading, extraction, resolution, package metadata handling, and MCP/library/CLI usage? [Completeness, Plan §Constraints, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior, Constitution §VII]
- [ ] CHK005 Are deterministic extraction requirements complete for AST/static-analysis-only graph structure, no LLM-derived graph data, stable repeated indexing, and fail-closed unsupported relationships? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-016, Plan §Constitution Check, Constitution §V]
- [ ] CHK006 Are permissive-asset requirements complete for every new grammar, WASM, vendored, generated, fixture, and documentation asset introduced by SPEC-023? [Completeness, Constitution §Fork & Ecosystem Constraints, Plan §Phase 0 Research]
- [ ] CHK007 Are package metadata parsing requirements complete enough to limit inputs to checked-in `dune-project`, `dune` stanzas, root `*.opam`, and `opam/*.opam` files while excluding `_opam`, lock directories, templates, installed switches, package repositories, and network state? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Research §Package metadata constrains local relationships only, Data Model §Workspace Metadata]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK008 Is grammar license evidence specified with objective fields rather than an untraceable statement that the grammar is permissive? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Research §Vendor tree-sitter-ocaml, GitHub tree-sitter-ocaml §LICENSE]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "no native runtime dependency" clarified so implementation cannot add compiled addons, native OCaml tooling, or runtime package-manager shells beyond existing pure-JS/WASM behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Constraints, Constitution §VII]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "no networked runtime behavior" clarified to distinguish allowed development-time corpus fetching or validation from normal CodeGraph runtime behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, Quickstart §4, Constitution §VII]
- [ ] CHK011 Is deterministic extraction defined with measurable evidence such as parser health checks, stable node/edge counts, no speculative edges, and repeated smoke outcomes? [Clarity, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-004, Quickstart §4]
- [ ] CHK012 Is "permissive" tied to the project's accepted license families, including MIT/Apache/BSD-compatible dependencies and no imported non-permissive code or text? [Clarity, Constitution §Fork & Ecosystem Constraints]
- [ ] CHK013 Is package metadata parsing clarified as reading checked-in declarative metadata only, without importing, executing, expanding, or copying dependency code into CodeGraph? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Data Model §Workspace Metadata, opam Manual §Package definitions, Dune Docs §dune-project]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK014 Do spec, plan, research, data model, contract, and quickstart agree that OCaml support ships through vendored `tree-sitter-ocaml@0.24.2` WASM/static assets copied by the existing build path? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Technical Context, Research §Vendor tree-sitter-ocaml, Contract §File Recognition and Status]
- [ ] CHK015 Do all artifacts consistently prohibit native runtime dependencies, installed switch state, package-manager runtime calls, and network package metadata? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Constraints, Research §Package metadata constrains local relationships only, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior]
- [ ] CHK016 Do all artifacts consistently prohibit package nodes, external package edges, and package relationships that require incompatible or non-local code? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Plan §Constraints, Data Model §OCaml Relationship, Contract §Non-Contract]
- [ ] CHK017 Do PPX requirements consistently state parse-preserved syntax only, with no generated-code import, PPX expansion, or speculative generated symbols? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, Research §PPX expansion is unsupported, Data Model §PPX Policy]
- [ ] CHK018 Do safety/license requirements stay aligned with the local-first constitution instead of weakening it for OCaml-specific package or grammar handling? [Consistency, Constitution §V, Constitution §VII, Spec §Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK019 Can copied-artifact success be objectively measured for both required OCaml WASMs without accepting a build that silently omits one artifact? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-005, Research §Health-check parser artifacts, Quickstart §1]
- [ ] CHK020 Can no-speculative-relationship success be objectively measured for ambiguous package, module, interface-pairing, and PPX cases? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior, Quickstart §3]
- [ ] CHK021 Can no-native and no-network runtime claims be objectively verified from task evidence, dependency review, and runtime behavior rather than assumed from design intent? [Acceptance Criteria, Plan §Phase 0 Research, Constitution §VII]
- [ ] CHK022 Can permissive-license compliance be objectively verified for all new assets, including grammar WASMs, provenance documentation, fixtures, and any vendored package metadata samples? [Acceptance Criteria, Constitution §Fork & Ecosystem Constraints, Plan §Review Packet Source]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK023 Are failure scenarios defined for missing WASM artifacts, parser initialization failure, incompatible grammar provenance, or a discovered non-permissive asset? [Coverage, Spec §Clarifications, Research §Health-check parser artifacts, Constitution §Fork & Ecosystem Constraints]
- [ ] CHK024 Are exception scenarios covered where checked-in Dune/opam metadata references external packages, installed switch state, templates, or generated code that must not become graph edges? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009, Data Model §Workspace Metadata, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior]
- [ ] CHK025 Are split-slice scenarios defined so early grammar/status or extraction slices cannot claim complete OCaml support before safety/license evidence and final validation gates are preserved? [Coverage, Plan §Implementation Slices, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK026 Are external source assumptions grounded in primary package/repository evidence for `tree-sitter-ocaml@0.24.2`, including the MIT license and the implementation/interface grammar exports? [Dependency, Spec §Clarifications, GitHub tree-sitter-ocaml §README, GitHub tree-sitter-ocaml §LICENSE]
- [ ] CHK027 Are Dune and opam metadata assumptions grounded in their public file-format documentation while remaining constrained to checked-in local files? [Dependency, Research §Package metadata constrains local relationships only, Dune Docs §dune-project, opam Manual §Package definitions]
- [ ] CHK028 Are safety/license decisions traceable to review-packet evidence so future task generation cannot omit grammar provenance, license review, dependency review, no-network behavior, deterministic extraction proof, or fail-closed package/PPX cases? [Traceability, Plan §Review Packet Source, Quickstart §8]
